calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Debra McKee appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mrs. McKee stated that she can't afford an attorney, that she needs to get the title of house from her mother's name and to her name, that she went to open door legal, to Bayview and to another place, but they won't help her, and that she is stressed out and having health issues because of this. Mrs. McKee also stated that City Hall told her that they cannot give her the original will and that they then told her that they could not find the original will. The court explained the need to have a court order to get the property transferred, and stated that the proof of publication of July 2023 does not indicate that Mrs. McKee is seeking to have a will admitted, that she needs to do re-publication and that she needs to provide all of the missing information. The court also suggested that Mrs. McKee call the bar association and provided her with their phone number. The Petition for Letters of Administration, and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary are off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-28-2023 Continued To Oct-16-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Aug-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Debra McKee, via Zoom, Petitioner, with daughter Norissa, via Zoom.

The status of the hiring of an attorney is discussed. Ms. McKee and her daughter advise that Open Door Legal may be willing to assist but that they would like to first review the Examiner's notes. Ms. McKee's daughter confirms her email address in open court, and requests that the Examiner's notes be forwarded to her to the email provided. The court advises that it is willing to allow Ms. McKee additional time considering Open Door Legal's possible involvement in this matter.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Aug-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-26-2023 Continued To Aug-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Debra McKee, via Zoom, Petitioner, with daughter Norissa, via Zoom.

The status of the hiring of an attorney is discussed. Ms. McKee and her daughter advise that Open Door Legal may be willing to assist but that they would like to first review the Examiner's notes. Ms. McKee's daughter confirms her email address in open court, and requests that the Examiner's notes be forwarded to her to the email provided. After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Aug-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-08-2023 Continued To Jun-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances; Debra Mckee (propria persona) appearing via zoom.
Norman Howard (propria persona) appearing via zoom.

The Court noted that a will has been brought forward and that it does not see a prospect for opening the estate intestate. Mr. Howard stated that his mother died 19 years ago, that he believes that Mrs. Mckee is not able to proceed without an attorney, expressed concern about the property deteriorating and being in deplorable condition (no heat, mold, stairs falling down) and Mrs. Mckee not having the financial resources/ability to repair the property. Mrs. Mckee spoke of the terms of the will, her finances and employment, and stated that open door legal was going to help her. The Court stated that Probate is very technical, that Mrs. Mckee really needs an attorney, and that Mr. Howard raises a fair point with respect to waiting 19 years. The Petition for Letters of Administration and Petition for Probate are continued to June 26 , 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to give Mrs. Mckee the opportunity to get an attorney. Mrs. Mckee is to inform Open Door Legal that she has to respond to the Probate examiners notes.

Judge; Ross Moody; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Choi; Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-14-2022 Continued To Mar-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Debra McKee, via Zoom, in propria persona, Petitioner; Norman Howard, Sr., via Zoom, Petitioner.

The court notes that Mr. Howard's attorney is not present today and Mr. Howard reports that he has not heard from him. Ms. McKee reports that she has found an attorney who advised her that her papers would not be ready until next March. The court advises Ms. McKee that she should consider contacting Open Door Legal to assist her with this matter.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Mar-15-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jul-19-2022 Continued To Jul-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-03-2022 Continued To Jul-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon, via Zoom, for Norman Howard, Petitioner; Debra McKee, in court, in propria persona, Interested Party.

The court points to the issue of the bond waiver filed on Mar-23-2022 specifically, the section that names Ms. McKee as the personal representative, signed by Ms. McKee. Mr. Simon reports that there is a typo in that document. Ms. McKee reports that she does not recall signing the document in question. Ms. McKee then reports that she has the original will in her possession and that she intended to file papers with the court but has had issues doing so. The court urges Ms. McKee to seek legal assistance. Mr. Simon reports that this is the first time he has heard that Ms. McKee has the original will. The court urges Ms. McKee and Mr. Simon to discuss this matter and advises that it will be granting one more continuance for Ms. McKee to seek legal advice and file a petition if applicable. The court advises that the court will not grant another continuance if progress has not been made by the next calling of the matter.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Jul-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-15-2022 Continued To May-03-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon via zoom for Norman Howard, via zoom, Petitioner; Debra McKee (decedent's daughter) and Norissa Stewart (decedent's niece) via zoom in propria persona; De Coty Phillips (decedent's nephew) and Vivian Gray Interested party, appeared in court in propria persona.

The court briefly summarizes the proceedings at the last calling of this matter and points Mr. Phillips to the fact that he did not formally file a competing petition therefore he currently has no standing in this matter. Mr. Phillips acknowledges the court's position and offers brief testimony regarding his position and the court advises that the facts offered by him are not before the court. Mr. Simon confirms that he does not have the original will in his possession. Ms. Stewart reports possibly giving the original will to her mom. Ms. McKee advises that she may have the will and plans to search for it. After additional discussion, the court advises the parties that it intends to continue this matter to allow Ms. McKee to seek legal counsel and file her own petition, if she wishes the court to consider her position regarding this matter. The court advises that the issue of whether this matter will proceed intestate or testate will need to be resolved.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May-03-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court orders that any competing petition or objection must be filed by Apr-26-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-16-2022 Continued To Mar-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon appeared by zoom for and with Norman Howard (petitioner) in court.
Debra McKee (decedent's daughter) appeared in court in propria persona.
De Coty Phillips (decedent's nephew) appeared in court in propria persona.

Mr. Simon stated that he was not aware of any objections, and that he was advised that De Coty wrote an ex parte letter, and that he was to appear. Mr. Philips stated that he did not receive notice. Mr. Simon stated that his understanding was that this was pretty straight forward, that the decedent died unmarried with two children, and that there is one asset which is one piece of real property in San Francisco. Mr. Phillips stated that he has objections to Howard being the administrator because that was not his Aunt Ruby's wish. Mr. Simon raised the issue of Mr. Phillips not having standing, and stated that only the daughter and the son have standing. Mr. Phillips presented a declaration and Ms. McKee presented a will. Both parties were instructed to file the documents and to serve them on everyone. Mr. Phillips stated that his Aunt told him that she wanted him to act on Debra's behalf no matter what. Mr. Simon stated that he does not have a will in his possession and that he has not seen the document. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission for the court to determine if the matter needs to be continued.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

Feb. 16, 2022:
Upon further consideration, the matter is continued to March 15, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
